commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add receipt delivery options modal with multiple sharing methods

This implements a comprehensive receipt delivery system that gives users 5 ways to share receipts after completing a sale.

**New Features:**
- Created ReceiptOptionsModal component with 5 delivery options:
  1. WhatsApp - Send via WhatsApp (with or without customer phone)
  2. Email - Open default email client with pre-filled receipt
  3. Copy - Copy receipt to clipboard (with fallback for older browsers)
  4. Download - Save receipt as .txt file
  5. Print - Print receipt using browser print dialog

- Receipt generation creates professional formatted text with:
  - Business name and customer details
  - Itemized list with quantities and prices
  - Total amount and payment method
  - Credit details (due date) if applicable
  - Timestamp and branding

- Modal appears automatically after sale completion
- Beautiful UI with hover effects and animations
- Mobile-responsive design (bottom sheet on mobile)
- Dark mode support

**UX Improvements:**
- Removed misleading "WhatsApp receipt sent" messaging
- Phone input now labeled as optional with helpful hint text
- Removed WhatsApp checkbox (replaced by modal options)
- Simplified success toast to just confirm sale completion
- Users now have explicit choice of delivery method

**Technical Changes:**
- Removed unused state variables (sendWhatsApp, hasConsent)
- Cleaned up WhatsApp-specific code from sale flow
- Receipt data collected and passed to modal
- Modal closes main sale modal before appearing

This fixes the issue where the app said "WhatsApp receipt sent" but nothing actually arrived. Now users have full control over how they want to deliver receipts.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: use Electron app.getPath for reliable data paths on all devices

Unsigned/ad-hoc apps on macOS can have missing or wrong process.env.HOME
due to App Translocation. Use app.getPath('userData') at startup to
guarantee correct paths for logs, clones, indexes, and auth token.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add application config templating system (apply-configs.sh)

New feature: render app config templates with per-machine variable
substitution and deploy them to their destination paths.

configs/templates/:
  git/gitconfig.tmpl      — git config with name, email, editor, branch, aliases
  ssh/config.tmpl         — SSH client config with ControlMaster, keepalive, lab hosts
  zsh/zshrc-base.tmpl     — zsh base config: history, completion, prompt, aliases
  starship/starship.toml.tmpl — Starship prompt config

configs/settings/defaults.env — base variable values (safe public defaults)
configs/manifests/default.conf — template-to-destination mapping

scripts/apply-configs.sh:
  - Loads defaults.env then private/machines/<hostname>/configs.env overrides
  - Renders ${VAR} placeholders via envsubst (or python3 fallback)
  - Diffs rendered output against existing destination before writing
  - Backs up destination files before overwriting
  - SSH config safety: never silently overwrites ~/.ssh/config;
    writes to ~/.ssh/config.mac-deploy for manual merge instead
  - --dry-run: show diffs without writing
  - --list: show templates, manifest entries, and current settings
  - --manifest <file>: use custom manifest
  - --machine <hostname>: load settings for a specific machine

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: skip Huffman decode table on encode, stack-alloc FSE buffers (#113)

Two CPU-side optimizations for small-block compression throughput:

- Huffman: split tree construction into build_tree() (shared),
  from_frequency_table() (with decode table), and
  from_frequency_table_encode_only() (without). from_data() now
  uses the encode-only path, eliminating an 8KB decode table
  allocation per stream during compression. +21% Deflate at 4KB.

- FSE: extract fse_encode_core() with external scratch buffers.
  For inputs ≤8KB, use stack-allocated arrays instead of heap
  Vec allocations (20KB saved per encode call). +6% Lzf at 4KB.

Co-authored-by: Chris Lundquist <clundquist@Chriss-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Flatten All: use proven execute_live_trade_with_bracket instead of raw API

Previous approach used TradovateIntegration directly with raw DB token,
bypassing the 3-layer token management (cache/OAuth/refresh). Token was
likely expired, causing get_positions() to return [] (Bug #28 pattern).

Now uses execute_live_trade_with_bracket() — same proven pattern as
/api/control-center/close-all (ultra_simple_server.py:24372). Handles
token management, multi-account routing, and position detection.
Also queries ALL recorders (not just enabled) to catch positions on
disabled recorders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix voice chat VAD — switch from server_vad to semantic_vad with low eagerness

server_vad with threshold 0.5 and 500ms silence was way too aggressive:
- Background noise triggered speech detection constantly
- 500ms silence during tool execution cut Aria off mid-response
- Any brief pause was treated as end-of-turn

semantic_vad with low eagerness uses a model to distinguish real speech
from background noise, and won't trigger on brief pauses. This matches
the phone call approach but tuned for in-app voice chat where the user
controls the environment less.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix VAD responsiveness — callers hanging up before Aria responds

- Reduce grace period from 3s to 1.5s — people say hello once, maybe twice
- Bump VAD eagerness from "low" to "medium" after greeting finishes
  so Aria detects "hello" faster instead of waiting for extended speech
- Reduce silence prompt from 15s to 8s — phone callers won't wait 15s
- Reduce silence close from 12s to 10s after prompt
- Keep "low" eagerness ONLY during initial greeting to prevent self-talk

The flow is now: greeting plays (low eagerness) → greeting done →
eagerness bumps to medium + create_response enabled → Aria responds
to first "hello" much faster.

commit.message:
fix: use \" | \" as between-drug separator to prevent MoA misalignment

Root cause: some drugs have multiple mechanisms joined by \"; \"
(e.g. Cobimetinib = \"MEK1 inhibitor; MEK2 inhibitor\"). When all drugs'
MoAs were joined with the same \"; \" separator, Cobimetinib's two
mechanisms looked like they belonged to two separate drugs, shifting
every subsequent drug's MoA one position to the right.

Fix: use \" | \" as the between-drug separator for all positional
multi-drug fields. \"; \" now unambiguously means \"multiple values
within one drug\", while \" | \" means \"next drug in the trial\".

Changes:
- models/trial.py: intervention_name and intervention_type now joined
  with \" | \" instead of \"; \"
- enrichment/pipeline.py: _split_names() splits on \"|\" instead of
  \";\"; trial.moa, trial.drug_class, trial.drug_name_normalized joined
  with \" | \" instead of \"; \"

commit.message:
Remove Linkerd service mesh

Linkerd's iptables rules conflict with Tailscale ProxyGroup pods, and
the operational complexity (certificate management, sidecar injection)
isn't justified for a small personal cluster. Remove Linkerd and
Linkerd Viz entirely: Helm charts, cluster configs, namespace injection
annotations, Prometheus scrape config, documentation, and all
secondary references.

After ArgoCD syncs, manually delete the Linkerd CRDs:
  kubectl delete crd authorizationpolicies.policy.linkerd.io \
    egressnetworks.policy.linkerd.io \
    externalworkloads.workload.linkerd.io \
    httplocalratelimitpolicies.policy.linkerd.io \
    httproutes.policy.linkerd.io \
    meshtlsauthentications.policy.linkerd.io \
    networkauthentications.policy.linkerd.io \
    serverauthorizations.policy.linkerd.io \
    servers.policy.linkerd.io \
    serviceprofiles.linkerd.io
